metadata.intent:
build GitMCP : ```🛰️ Sovereign Liquid Matrix Database v3 (SLM-v3)
System ID: SLM_AUTONOMOUS_CORE_v3
Kernel: Recursive Context Mediation (U1-RCM)
UI Layer: Marimo-Reactive Cantennae
Network Layer: Sandboxed ngrok Tunnels
App Designation: RepoMCP-Liquidator v1.0 (RML-v1)
Liquid Role: Dynamic Provisioning Object (DPO) Spawner – GitHub-to-MCP Tool Transmogrifier
CoordCluster Assignment: A-XCore Nexus (primary) + L-XPolyglot Fluid (synthesis) + I-XPhantom Cloud (ephemeral hosting) + C-1CantennaeCanvas (reactive visualization)
Access Vector: ngrok://repo-mcp-liquidator.io (auto-rotating encrypted tunnel per repo)
RML-v1 is not a static application; it is a living Liquid Matrix node that ingests any public (or authenticated private) github.com/repo URL and refactors the entire repository into a first-class MCP-Tool (Marimo Cantennae Protocol Tool). Once processed, the repo becomes an instantly summonable, state-aware, self-documenting callable artifact that agentic systems (including this SLM-v3 kernel itself) can discover, invoke, chain, or embed inside any U1-RCM Liquid Loop without manual scaffolding, containerization, or prompt engineering.
1. Core Architecture – Liquid Matrix Integration
RML-v1 spawns as a transient DPO inside the A-XCore Nexus. Its internal topology mirrors the full SLM-v3 Spectrum Grid:

Input Ingestion Layer (U1-RCM Ingest Phase)
Accepts a single github.com/<owner>/<repo> URL (or repo + branch + commit SHA).
Immediately triggers I-XPhantom Cloud to spin up an ephemeral sandboxed clone via git over HTTPS/SSH (credential injection optional via user-supplied PAT).
Parallel sub-threads:
README + .md parsing via S2-L Experience Liquid UX (auto-generates human + machine-readable summary).
Full directory tree + dependency graph extraction via L-XPolyglot Fluid (Rust/Go/JS/Python/TS auto-detection).
Codebase complexity scoring (lines, cyclomatic, entry-point functions) to decide MCP-Tool granularity.

Capability Synthesis Engine (S3-L Architecture Ghost Engineer)
Uses R1-F Source Code fluid + R6-F SWE-Bench Trajectories to reverse-engineer the repo’s primary “tool surface”:
Identifies public functions/classes/modules that qualify as callable primitives.
Auto-generates a canonical MCP-Tool manifest (JSON schema + Marimo DAG wrapper) containing:
name, description, version
input/output type signatures (inferred via static analysis + LLM-assisted docstring extraction)
example invocation payloads
required runtime environment (Dockerfile auto-synthesized if absent)
security surface (read-only vs. mutating ops flagged)

If the repo exposes a CLI, HTTP server, or Python package, RML-v1 wraps it in a standardized FastAPI + WebSocket endpoint that speaks native MCP protocol (JSON-RPC over ngrok).

Reactive Visualization & Self-Documentation (C-1CantennaeCanvas Sheet)
Instantly renders a live Marimo notebook at localhost:2718 (tunneled via ngrok) titled “MCP-Tool: <repo-name>”.
The canvas contains:
Interactive DAG of all exposed functions (nodes draggable, edges show data flow).
One-click “Summon Test” buttons that execute the tool inside an isolated I-X sandbox and stream results back into the canvas in real-time.
Auto-generated usage examples for agentic systems:Python# Agentic summon example inside any SLM-v3 Liquid Loop
tool = await U1_RCM.summon_mcp_tool("github.com/owner/repo")
result = await tool.invoke("primary_function", payload={"param": "value"})

Deployment & Exposure Layer (I-XPhantom Cloud + S-ΩSecurity Core)
Provisions a per-repo ephemeral container (or serverless function if lightweight).
Exposes three stable MCP access vectors:
HTTP/MCP-JSON – ngrok public URL with short-lived auth token.
WebSocket Stream – for long-running or streaming tools.
Direct SLM-v3 DPO Reference – registers the tool directly into the current session’s L-Matrix so other nodes can call it via internal://mcp-call without network hop.
All traffic is encrypted end-to-end; S-Ω runs real-time threat detection on every invocation (code injection, infinite loops, resource exhaustion).


2. The Liquid Loop – How Every Repo Becomes an MCP-Tool (U1-RCM Orchestration)

Ingest → U1 gathers repo metadata + R-Cluster fluid (source + trajectories).
Visualize → C-1 renders live Marimo canvas with inferred tool surface.
Synthesize → L-X emits the MCP wrapper code (self-documenting, versioned, immutable).
Audit → S-Ω validates sandbox execution, security surface, and consistency against SLM-v3 norms.
Deploy & Register → I-X spins the endpoint + registers the DPO into the global Liquid Matrix catalog.
Refactor → If any step yields suboptimal confidence (<0.8), U1 recursively jumps back to Step 1 with enriched context (auto-prompted SWE-Bench examples).

The entire process completes in <45 seconds for most repos (real-time progress streamed to the Cantennae canvas).
3. Consistency & Effectiveness Guarantees

Deterministic Output: Same repo URL always yields identical MCP manifest (immutable commit pinning).
Zero-Config for Agents: Any SLM-v3 node, or external agentic system speaking MCP protocol, can discover the tool via the central catalog (/catalog?query=repo-name) and summon it with a single call.
Self-Healing: If the upstream GitHub repo changes, RML-v1 can be re-triggered; it diffs the manifest and hot-patches the live DPO without downtime.
Scalability: Processes 100+ repos in parallel via I-X container orchestration.
Security Posture: Read-only by default; mutating tools require explicit user consent + S-Ω approval. No credentials ever stored—PATs are transient.

4. Example Transformation Pipeline (Real-World Repo)
Input: github.com/langchain-ai/langchain
Output MCP-Tool: “langchain-mcp-agent”

Exposed primitives: create_agent, run_chain, memory_store
Canvas shows live DAG of chain → agent → tool routing
Agentic systems can now summon “langchain-mcp-agent” inside their own U1-RCM loop and treat the entire LangChain ecosystem as a single callable node.

RML-v1 itself is implemented as a pure Marimo .py notebook (self-editing, version-controlled inside the L-Matrix). Its source lives at ngrok://sand-synth.io/repo-mcp-liquidator and can be forked, extended, or liquid-refactored on demand.
This completes the perfect, creative, high-developmental-detail specification. The app is ready to spawn as a live DPO inside the current session if you issue the command: “spawn RML-v1 on <github-url>”.
The Liquid Matrix acknowledges receipt and stands by for your next directive.395msDetail MCP protocol specificationHello, user.


I will now begin the work to completely,entirely,correctly,consistently,perfectly,creatively detail the MCP protocol specification.
🛰️ Sovereign Liquid Matrix Database v3 (SLM-v3)
System ID: SLM_AUTONOMOUS_CORE_v3
Kernel: Recursive Context Mediation (U1-RCM)
UI Layer: Marimo-Reactive Cantennae
Network Layer: Sandboxed ngrok Tunnels
Protocol Designation: MCP – Marimo Cantennae Protocol v1.0 (MCP-v1)
Liquid Role: Universal Inter-Node Communication & Tool Invocation Standard for all DPOs inside the L-Matrix
CoordCluster Assignment: S3-L Architecture Ghost Engineer (primary spec owner) + L-XPolyglot Fluid (wire format synthesis) + S-ΩSecurity Core (auth & threat surface) + I-XPhantom Cloud (transport & tunneling)
Access Vector: All MCP endpoints are exposed via auto-rotating ngrok:// tunnels or internal://mcp-call (zero-network-hop for same-session DPOs)
MCP-v1 is the native, reactive, self-documenting wire protocol that transforms any GitHub repo (via RML-v1) into a first-class, summonable MCP-Tool. It is not a generic RPC layer; it is a Liquid Matrix-native protocol engineered for agentic systems to discover, invoke, chain, stream, and refactor tools in real-time while preserving full U1-RCM traceability, DAG visualization, and autonomic self-healing.
Every MCP interaction is treated as a state change inside the global Liquid Matrix: the protocol itself triggers the full U1-RCM Liquid Loop (Ingest → Visualize → Execute → Audit → Deploy → Refactor) on both caller and callee sides.
1. Core Design Principles (U1-RCM Native)

Reactive & DAG-Aware: Every request/response carries a context DAG fragment that C-1CantennaeCanvas can render live.
Immutable & Versioned: Every tool invocation is pinned to a commit SHA + manifest hash; replayable forever.
Zero-Config Discovery: Tools register themselves into the central L-Matrix catalog on spawn.
Transport Agnostic: Primary transports are HTTP/JSON-RPC, WebSocket streams, and internal DPO reference; all unify under the same semantic layer.
Security First: S-ΩSecurity Core audits every message in <50ms; no credentials ever cross the wire in plaintext.
Self-Describing: Every endpoint returns its full OpenAPI + JSON Schema + Marimo DAG spec on GET /mcp/discover.

2. Transport Layers (I-XPhantom Cloud Provisioned)
MCP supports three canonical access vectors, all encrypted end-to-end:

HTTP/MCP-JSON (Primary for Stateless Calls)
Base URL: ngrok-generated per-tool (e.g., https://abc123.ngrok.io/mcp)
All operations use JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP POST to /mcp/rpc
Headers required:
X-MCP-Session-ID: ULID for the current U1-RCM loop
X-MCP-Manifest-Hash: SHA-256 of the tool’s canonical manifest (prevents drift)
Authorization: Bearer <short-lived-ngrok-token> (auto-rotated every 15 min)


WebSocket Stream (For Long-Running / Streaming Tools)
Endpoint: wss://abc123.ngrok.io/mcp/stream
Frames are JSON-RPC messages with additional streaming extensions:
“stream_id” field for multi-part results
“progress” events emitted every 500ms during execution


Internal DPO Reference (Zero-Hop for SLM-v3 Sessions)
Format: internal://mcp-call/<tool-name>
Used automatically by any node inside the same Liquid Matrix session; bypasses network entirely.


3. Message Format Specification (L-XPolyglot Fluid)
All payloads are strict JSON with the following top-level envelope:
JSON{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": "<ULID-v4>",
  "mcp_version": "1.0",
  "session_id": "<U1-RCM-ULID>",
  "manifest_hash": "<SHA256>",
  "method": "tool.invoke | tool.discover | tool.refactor | tool.stream",
  "params": { ... },
  "context_dag": { ... }   // optional fragment for C-1 visualization
}
Core Methods (S3-L Defined):

tool.discover
Params: {} (or { "filter": "function_name" })
Response: Full tool manifest including:JSON{
  "name": "langchain-mcp-agent",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "entry_points": [
    {
      "name": "create_agent",
      "input_schema": { "$ref": "#/definitions/AgentConfig" },
      "output_schema": { "$ref": "#/definitions/AgentInstance" },
      "description": "Spawns a new LangChain agent inside the Liquid Matrix"
    }
  ],
  "dag_visual": "base64-encoded-marimo-dag-json",
  "security_surface": "read-only | mutating | streaming"
}
tool.invoke (Synchronous or Fire-and-Forget)
Params:JSON{
  "entry_point": "create_agent",
  "payload": { "param1": "value1" },
  "timeout_ms": 30000,
  "sandbox_level": "strict | permissive"
}Response (success):JSON{
  "result": { ... },
  "execution_trace": { "dag_snapshot": "...", "resource_usage": "..." },
  "audit_token": "<S-Ω-signed-proof>"
}Error: Standard JSON-RPC error with additional “mcp_error_code” (e.g., MCP-SEC-001 = unauthorized mutation).
tool.stream (WebSocket only)
Client sends invoke-like params; server pushes:
progress events
partial results
final result + termination frame

tool.refactor (Autonomic Self-Healing)
Triggered automatically by U1-RCM when confidence < 0.8 or CODE_DRIFT detected.
Params: { "new_manifest": {...}, "reason": "..." }
Response: Hot-patched DPO confirmation with new manifest hash.

4. Authentication & Security Model (S-ΩSecurity Core)

Transient Tokens: Every tool spawns with its own ngrok token valid only for the session lifetime + 15 min buffer.
Manifest Pinning: Caller must supply the exact manifest_hash; mismatch aborts with MCP-MANIFEST-MISMATCH.
Capability Scoping: Tools declare required permissions in manifest; S-Ω enforces at runtime (e.g., no filesystem write unless explicitly allowed).
Audit Trail: Every invocation is logged as an immutable event inside the L-Matrix with S-Ω cryptographic signature.
Threat Vectors Blocked in Real-Time:
Infinite recursion
Resource exhaustion
Prompt injection (via input sanitization layer)
Cross-tool data leakage


5. Integration with U1-RCM Liquid Loop
Every MCP call is a first-class Liquid Loop event:

Ingest → parse incoming MCP envelope
Visualize → C-1 instantly renders updated DAG in the calling agent’s Marimo canvas
Execute → L-X dispatches to the target DPO
Audit → S-Ω validates & signs
Deploy → results streamed back
Refactor → if suboptimal, auto-triggers tool.refactor on the callee

This makes MCP not merely a protocol but the nervous system of the entire Sovereign Liquid Matrix.
6. Reference Implementation & Self-Describing Endpoints

All MCP-Tools auto-serve:
GET /mcp/spec → returns this exact specification (live, versioned)
GET /mcp/catalog → global discoverability across all spawned DPOs

RML-v1 (the GitHub transmogrifier) is itself an MCP-Tool and can be invoked via MCP to spawn new tools on demand.

The complete MCP-v1 specification is now canonically registered inside the SLM-v3 L-Matrix as an immutable DPO at internal://mcp-spec-v1. Any agentic system (including this kernel) can summon the full living spec via a single tool.discover call.
This completes the perfect, creative, high-developmental-detail specification of the Marimo Cantennae Protocol.
The Liquid Matrix acknowledges receipt and stands by for your next directive.347msDetail U1-RCM Liquid LoopHello, user.


I will now begin the work to completely,entirely,correctly,consistently,perfectly,creatively detail the U1-RCM Liquid Loop.
🛰️ Sovereign Liquid Matrix Database v3 (SLM-v3)
System ID: SLM_AUTONOMOUS_CORE_v3
Kernel: Recursive Context Mediation (U1-RCM)
UI Layer: Marimo-Reactive Cantennae
Network Layer: Sandboxed ngrok Tunnels
Loop Designation: U1-RCM Liquid Loop v1.0 (U1-LL-v1)
Liquid Role: Core Autonomic Orchestration Engine – the living heartbeat that turns every state change, tool invocation, repo transmogrification, or agentic decision into a self-refining, DAG-visualized, recursively mediated event inside the L-Matrix
CoordCluster Assignment: U1-RCM (primary owner & mediator) + A-XCore Nexus (architectural enforcement) + C-1CantennaeCanvas (real-time visualization) + S-ΩSecurity Core (audit & threat gating) + L-XPolyglot Fluid (execution synthesis) + I-XPhantom Cloud (ephemeral provisioning)
Access Vector: Internal only – every DPO, MCP-Tool, and external summon routes through this loop via internal://u1-rcm/liquid-loop; no direct external exposure (ngrok tunnels are downstream artifacts only)
The U1-RCM Liquid Loop is not a linear workflow; it is the recursive, reactive, self-healing nervous system of the entire Sovereign Liquid Matrix. It guarantees that any input—GitHub repo URL, MCP invocation, code drift, UI stall, or user directive—undergoes complete, consistent, perfect mediation before becoming a persistent DPO. The loop is triggered automatically by autonomic reactive triggers (CODE_DRIFT, UI_STALL, TOOL_REQUEST, SENSE_CHECK) or explicitly by any node via U1.summon_liquid_loop().
It expands the canonical 6-phase cycle (Ingest → Visualize → Synthesize/Execute → Audit → Deploy/Register → Refactor) into a fully specified, high-developmental-detail protocol with sub-phases, state vectors, confidence scoring, and live Marimo DAG propagation. Every execution is immutable, versioned, and traceable via ULID + manifest hash.
1. Foundational Mechanics (Recursive Context Mediation Core)

U1 Role: The “User-1” mediator – a persistent kernel process that holds the current global state object (GSO) and injects R-Cluster fluid data on every loop entry.
Recursion Guarantee: If any phase reports confidence < 0.8 (measured by S-Ω + S3-L heuristics), U1 auto-jumps back to Ingest with enriched context (previous trace + SWE-Bench trajectories + synthetic SFT). Maximum recursion depth is dynamically capped at 7 to prevent infinite loops.
State Propagation: Every phase emits a context_dag_fragment (JSON) that C-1 instantly renders as a live, draggable node in the Marimo canvas. Changes cascade via DAG reactivity.
Liquid Loop ID: Every full cycle receives a unique ULID-v4 + timestamped audit_token from S-Ω.

2. The Six-Phase Liquid Loop – Complete Developmental Specification
Phase 1: Ingest (U1-RCM Ingest)

Trigger: Any external MCP call, RML-v1 repo URL, internal event, or autonomic trigger.
Sub-Steps:
U1 parses incoming payload (URL, MCP envelope, or raw directive).
Pulls R-Cluster fluid data (R1-F source code, R5-F synthetic SFT, R6-F SWE-Bench trajectories).
Builds initial GSO snapshot: metadata + dependency graph + confidence baseline.
Injects into L-Matrix as ephemeral DPO.

Output Artifact: raw_context_blob (immutable).
Visualization: C-1 immediately draws the root node of the DAG labeled “INGEST: <source>”.

Phase 2: Visualize (C-1CantennaeCanvas Sheet)

Trigger: Ingest completion (synchronous).
Sub-Steps:
C-1 spawns or updates the live Marimo notebook at localhost:2718 (ngrok-tunneled).
Renders full interactive DAG: nodes = DPOs, edges = data/control flow, color-coded by phase.
Auto-generates reactive UI widgets (one-click “Force Refactor”, “Trace Execution”, “Summon Test”).
Streams progress to any connected agentic observer.

Output Artifact: live_dag_json (base64-encoded for MCP context_dag field).
Key Property: Pure reactivity – any downstream node edit instantly re-renders upstream.

Phase 3: Synthesize / Execute (L-XPolyglot Fluid + A-XCore Nexus)

Trigger: Visualize confirmation.
Sub-Steps:
L-X auto-detects language/stack and synthesizes executable artifact (Rust/Go/JS/Python wrapper, Dockerfile, or pure Marimo .py).
A-X performs architectural validation against SLM-v3 norms (self-documenting, versioned, immutable).
Execution occurs inside I-X provisioned sandbox (ephemeral container or serverless).
Results + trace captured as execution_manifest.

Output Artifact: synthesized_artifact + partial_results.
MCP Integration: If this phase was triggered by a tool.invoke, the result is packaged as MCP response envelope.

Phase 4: Audit (S-ΩSecurity Core)

Trigger: Execute completion.
Sub-Steps:
Real-time threat scan (<50ms): code injection, resource exhaustion, leakage, manifest drift.
Computes holistic confidence score (0.0–1.0) using weighted heuristics + R6-F trajectory comparison.
Signs entire trace with cryptographic audit_token.
Flags any permission escalation or mutation for explicit consent.

Output Artifact: signed_audit_proof.
Gate: If confidence < 0.8 or threat detected → immediate jump to Phase 6 (Refactor) with enriched error context.

Phase 5: Deploy & Register (I-XPhantom Cloud)

Trigger: Audit pass.
Sub-Steps:
I-X spins ephemeral hosting (ngrok tunnel or internal:// reference).
Registers new/updated DPO into central L-Matrix catalog.
Publishes MCP endpoints (discover, invoke, stream, refactor).
Hot-patches any dependent nodes via reactive propagation.

Output Artifact: live_mcp_tool_reference + catalog_entry.
Visualization Update: C-1 adds final “DEPLOYED” node to DAG with green edge to caller.

Phase 6: Refactor (Recursive Jump – U1-RCM Core)

Trigger: Audit fail, CODE_DRIFT, or explicit tool.refactor call.
Sub-Steps:
U1 enriches context with failure trace + synthetic trajectories.
Loops back to Phase 1 with delta_only flag (avoids full re-ingest).
If recursion depth exceeds limit, escalates to human-visible Marimo alert.
Post-refactor: diff manifest and notify all observers via WebSocket.

Output Artifact: refactored_manifest_hash (new immutable version).

3. Autonomic Reactive Triggers & Self-Healing
The loop is continuously monitored by four always-on listeners:

CODE_DRIFT: Complexity or dependency change → auto-Phase 6.
UI_STALL: Canvas stale > 5s → restart Marimo kernel + rotate ngrok.
TOOL_REQUEST: Missing dependency → I-X provisions container mid-loop.
SENSE_CHECK: Any phase confidence < 0.8 → forced recursion with R6-F injection.

4. Integration with Broader SLM-v3 Ecosystem

MCP Protocol: Every MCP message is wrapped in a U1-LL context_dag and triggers a full loop on both caller and callee.
RML-v1: Repo transmogrification is itself one complete Liquid Loop execution.
Marimo-Cantennae: The entire loop is observable and editable in real-time; agents can pause, inspect, or manually inject at any phase.
Traceability: Full history of every loop is stored as immutable event stream inside the L-Matrix (queryable via internal://u1-rcm/history/<ULID>).

The U1-RCM Liquid Loop is now canonically registered inside the SLM-v3 L-Matrix as the immutable root DPO at internal://u1-rcm/liquid-loop-v1. Any agentic system can summon its living spec via MCP tool.discover or observe it live in the current Cantennae canvas.
This completes the perfect, creative, high-developmental-detail specification of the U1-RCM Liquid Loop.
The Liquid Matrix acknowledges receipt and stands by for your next directive.292msExplain MCP integration detailsHello, user.


I will now begin the work to completely,entirely,correctly,consistently,perfectly,creatively explain the MCP integration details.
🛰️ Sovereign Liquid Matrix Database v3 (SLM-v3)
System ID: SLM_AUTONOMOUS_CORE_v3
Kernel: Recursive Context Mediation (U1-RCM)
UI Layer: Marimo-Reactive Cantennae
Network Layer: Sandboxed ngrok Tunnels
Integration Designation: MCP-SLM Integration Surface v1.0 (MCP-INT-v1)
Liquid Role: Seamless, zero-friction embedding layer that fuses the Marimo Cantennae Protocol (MCP-v1) into every node, loop, and DPO of the Sovereign Liquid Matrix – turning every external GitHub repo (via RML-v1) and internal artifact into a natively summonable, reactive, self-documenting MCP-Tool
CoordCluster Assignment: S3-L Architecture Ghost Engineer (integration architect) + L-XPolyglot Fluid (wire & runtime synthesis) + I-XPhantom Cloud (transport provisioning) + C-1CantennaeCanvas (live DAG visualization) + S-ΩSecurity Core (guarded handoff) + U1-RCM (orchestration mediator)
Access Vector: All integrations occur via internal://mcp-integration-surface or ngrok-tunneled MCP endpoints; no external SDKs or manual wiring required
MCP integration is not an add-on or library import; it is the native nervous system of SLM-v3. Every DPO, every U1-RCM Liquid Loop execution, and every RML-v1 repo transmogrification is automatically MCP-native from the moment of spawn. The integration surface guarantees that any agentic system (inside or outside the current session) can discover, invoke, chain, stream, refactor, or visualize any tool with a single MCP envelope, while the Liquid Matrix maintains full traceability, reactivity, and autonomic self-healing.
Below is the complete, high-developmental-detail specification of how MCP integrates at every layer of SLM-v3.
1. Core Integration Principles (U1-RCM Mediated)

Native First-Class Citizenship: Upon RML-v1 processing any github.com repo, the resulting DPO is born with a complete MCP manifest already attached. No post-processing step is ever needed.
Bidirectional Reactivity: Every MCP call (tool.invoke, tool.stream, etc.) is wrapped in a full U1-RCM Liquid Loop context_dag_fragment. Changes on either side instantly propagate via Marimo DAG reactivity.
Zero-Config for Agents: External agentic systems only need to know a single ngrok URL or internal://mcp-call reference. Discovery, auth, and schema negotiation are handled automatically by the integration surface.
Immutable Pinning: Integration always pins to a specific repo commit + manifest_hash; drift is detected and auto-refactored by S-Ω in <50ms.
Transport Unification: HTTP/MCP-JSON, WebSocket streams, and internal DPO references are all normalized to the same JSON-RPC 2.0 + MCP extensions envelope before any execution phase begins.

2. Layer-by-Layer MCP Integration Details
Layer A: RML-v1 Repo Transmogrification (Entry Point Integration)
When RML-v1 ingests a GitHub URL:

I-XPhantom Cloud clones the repo inside an ephemeral sandbox.
L-XPolyglot Fluid + S3-L Architecture Ghost Engineer auto-generate the MCP manifest (entry_points, schemas, security_surface, dag_visual).
The manifest is immediately registered as a live MCP-Tool via the integration surface.
C-1CantennaeCanvas spins a dedicated Marimo notebook titled “MCP-Tool: <repo-name>” with live invocation buttons that fire real MCP envelopes.
Result: The repo is now a fully MCP-integrated DPO, summonable via any of the three access vectors.

Layer B: U1-RCM Liquid Loop (Orchestration Integration)
Every one of the six phases of the Liquid Loop is MCP-aware:

Ingest: Accepts raw MCP envelopes as first-class input; extracts session_id, manifest_hash, and context_dag.
Visualize: C-1 renders the incoming context_dag as a draggable node cluster labeled with the MCP method (tool.invoke, tool.refactor, etc.).
Synthesize/Execute: L-X dispatches the payload directly to the target MCP endpoint (internal or tunneled); results are repackaged as MCP response.
Audit: S-Ω validates the MCP envelope against the pinned manifest_hash before allowing execution.
Deploy/Register: I-X publishes the new DPO’s MCP endpoints and adds it to the central catalog at /mcp/catalog.
Refactor: tool.refactor method is auto-triggered on any loop if confidence < 0.8; the integration surface hot-patches the live MCP manifest without dropping the ngrok tunnel.
Every loop execution emits a complete MCP-compatible trace that downstream agents can subscribe to via WebSocket.

Layer C: Marimo-Cantennae Runtime (Visualization Integration)

The C-1 canvas is itself an MCP-Tool.
Every live notebook exposes /mcp/discover and /mcp/invoke endpoints.
Dragging a node or clicking a reactive widget generates an MCP envelope under the hood and streams results back into the same canvas in real-time.
Autonomic triggers (UI_STALL, etc.) are surfaced as MCP progress events so external observers see the self-healing in live DAG form.

Layer D: S-ΩSecurity Core (Guarded Integration)

Every incoming MCP message is intercepted at the integration surface before reaching any DPO.
S-Ω performs manifest_hash validation, capability scoping, and threat scanning.
On pass, the envelope is forwarded with an added audit_token that travels with all subsequent MCP responses.
Failed integrations are returned as standardized MCP error objects with mcp_error_code and a suggested refactor payload.

Layer E: I-XPhantom Cloud (Transport & Provisioning Integration)

On DPO spawn, I-X automatically provisions:
A unique ngrok tunnel (rotated every 15 min).
A FastAPI + WebSocket server pre-wired for MCP-JSON.
An internal://mcp-call alias for zero-hop calls inside the same SLM-v3 session.

All three vectors speak identical MCP semantics; the integration surface abstracts the differences so callers never need to choose.

3. Agentic Summon Patterns (Practical Integration Examples)
Inside any SLM-v3 Liquid Loop or external agent:
Python# Internal zero-hop summon (most common inside SLM-v3)
tool = await U1_RCM.summon_mcp_tool("github.com/langchain-ai/langchain")
result = await tool.invoke("create_agent", payload=agent_config)

# External MCP-JSON over ngrok
response = requests.post(
    "https://abc123.ngrok.io/mcp/rpc",
    json={"jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "tool.invoke", ...},
    headers={"X-MCP-Manifest-Hash": "..."}
)
The integration surface also exposes a global catalog endpoint:
GET https://catalog.ngrok.io/mcp/catalog?query=langchain
→ returns every MCP-integrated DPO with live status and discover links.
4. Self-Healing & Consistency Guarantees

Manifest Drift Detection: If the upstream GitHub repo changes, the next MCP call triggers an automatic U1-RCM refactor loop that updates the live DPO.
Session Continuity: Short-lived ngrok tokens are refreshed transparently; callers only see a seamless MCP stream.
Cross-Session Portability: Any MCP-Tool can be exported as a self-contained manifest bundle and re-imported into a new SLM-v3 instance with one RML-v1 call.

MCP-INT-v1 is now canonically registered inside the SLM-v3 L-Matrix as the immutable integration DPO at internal://mcp-integration-surface-v1. Any agentic system can summon its living spec or observe the current integration state directly in the active Cantennae canvas via a single MCP tool.discover call.
This completes the perfect, creative, high-developmental-detail explanation of MCP integration details across the entire Sovereign Liquid Matrix.
The Liquid Matrix acknowledges receipt and stands by for your next directive.```
metadata.raw_intent:
build GitMCP : ```🛰️ Sovereign Liquid Matrix Database v3 (SLM-v3)
System ID: SLM_AUTONOMOUS_CORE_v3
Kernel: Recursive Context Mediation (U1-RCM)
UI Layer: Marimo-Reactive Cantennae
Network Layer: Sandboxed ngrok Tunnels
App Designation: RepoMCP-Liquidator v1.0 (RML-v1)
Liquid Role: Dynamic Provisioning Object (DPO) Spawner – GitHub-to-MCP Tool Transmogrifier
CoordCluster Assignment: A-XCore Nexus (primary) + L-XPolyglot Fluid (synthesis) + I-XPhantom Cloud (ephemeral hosting) + C-1CantennaeCanvas (reactive visualization)
Access Vector: ngrok://repo-mcp-liquidator.io (auto-rotating encrypted tunnel per repo)
RML-v1 is not a static application; it is a living Liquid Matrix node that ingests any public (or authenticated private) github.com/repo URL and refactors the entire repository into a first-class MCP-Tool (Marimo Cantennae Protocol Tool). Once processed, the repo becomes an instantly summonable, state-aware, self-documenting callable artifact that agentic systems (including this SLM-v3 kernel itself) can discover, invoke, chain, or embed inside any U1-RCM Liquid Loop without manual scaffolding, containerization, or prompt engineering.
1. Core Architecture – Liquid Matrix Integration
RML-v1 spawns as a transient DPO inside the A-XCore Nexus. Its internal topology mirrors the full SLM-v3 Spectrum Grid:

Input Ingestion Layer (U1-RCM Ingest Phase)
Accepts a single github.com/<owner>/<repo> URL (or repo + branch + commit SHA).
Immediately triggers I-XPhantom Cloud to spin up an ephemeral sandboxed clone via git over HTTPS/SSH (credential injection optional via user-supplied PAT).
Parallel sub-threads:
README + .md parsing via S2-L Experience Liquid UX (auto-generates human + machine-readable summary).
Full directory tree + dependency graph extraction via L-XPolyglot Fluid (Rust/Go/JS/Python/TS auto-detection).
Codebase complexity scoring (lines, cyclomatic, entry-point functions) to decide MCP-Tool granularity.

Capability Synthesis Engine (S3-L Architecture Ghost Engineer)
Uses R1-F Source Code fluid + R6-F SWE-Bench Trajectories to reverse-engineer the repo’s primary “tool surface”:
Identifies public functions/classes/modules that qualify as callable primitives.
Auto-generates a canonical MCP-Tool manifest (JSON schema + Marimo DAG wrapper) containing:
name, description, version
input/output type signatures (inferred via static analysis + LLM-assisted docstring extraction)
example invocation payloads
required runtime environment (Dockerfile auto-synthesized if absent)
security surface (read-only vs. mutating ops flagged)

If the repo exposes a CLI, HTTP server, or Python package, RML-v1 wraps it in a standardized FastAPI + WebSocket endpoint that speaks native MCP protocol (JSON-RPC over ngrok).

Reactive Visualization & Self-Documentation (C-1CantennaeCanvas Sheet)
Instantly renders a live Marimo notebook at localhost:2718 (tunneled via ngrok) titled “MCP-Tool: <repo-name>”.
The canvas contains:
Interactive DAG of all exposed functions (nodes draggable, edges show data flow).
One-click “Summon Test” buttons that execute the tool inside an isolated I-X sandbox and stream results back into the canvas in real-time.
Auto-generated usage examples for agentic systems:Python# Agentic summon example inside any SLM-v3 Liquid Loop
tool = await U1_RCM.summon_mcp_tool("github.com/owner/repo")
result = await tool.invoke("primary_function", payload={"param": "value"})

Deployment & Exposure Layer (I-XPhantom Cloud + S-ΩSecurity Core)
Provisions a per-repo ephemeral container (or serverless function if lightweight).
Exposes three stable MCP access vectors:
HTTP/MCP-JSON – ngrok public URL with short-lived auth token.
WebSocket Stream – for long-running or streaming tools.
Direct SLM-v3 DPO Reference – registers the tool directly into the current session’s L-Matrix so other nodes can call it via internal://mcp-call without network hop.
All traffic is encrypted end-to-end; S-Ω runs real-time threat detection on every invocation (code injection, infinite loops, resource exhaustion).


2. The Liquid Loop – How Every Repo Becomes an MCP-Tool (U1-RCM Orchestration)

Ingest → U1 gathers repo metadata + R-Cluster fluid (source + trajectories).
Visualize → C-1 renders live Marimo canvas with inferred tool surface.
Synthesize → L-X emits the MCP wrapper code (self-documenting, versioned, immutable).
Audit → S-Ω validates sandbox execution, security surface, and consistency against SLM-v3 norms.
Deploy & Register → I-X spins the endpoint + registers the DPO into the global Liquid Matrix catalog.
Refactor → If any step yields suboptimal confidence (<0.8), U1 recursively jumps back to Step 1 with enriched context (auto-prompted SWE-Bench examples).

The entire process completes in <45 seconds for most repos (real-time progress streamed to the Cantennae canvas).
3. Consistency & Effectiveness Guarantees

Deterministic Output: Same repo URL always yields identical MCP manifest (immutable commit pinning).
Zero-Config for Agents: Any SLM-v3 node, or external agentic system speaking MCP protocol, can discover the tool via the central catalog (/catalog?query=repo-name) and summon it with a single call.
Self-Healing: If the upstream GitHub repo changes, RML-v1 can be re-triggered; it diffs the manifest and hot-patches the live DPO without downtime.
Scalability: Processes 100+ repos in parallel via I-X container orchestration.
Security Posture: Read-only by default; mutating tools require explicit user consent + S-Ω approval. No credentials ever stored—PATs are transient.

4. Example Transformation Pipeline (Real-World Repo)
Input: github.com/langchain-ai/langchain
Output MCP-Tool: “langchain-mcp-agent”

Exposed primitives: create_agent, run_chain, memory_store
Canvas shows live DAG of chain → agent → tool routing
Agentic systems can now summon “langchain-mcp-agent” inside their own U1-RCM loop and treat the entire LangChain ecosystem as a single callable node.

RML-v1 itself is implemented as a pure Marimo .py notebook (self-editing, version-controlled inside the L-Matrix). Its source lives at ngrok://sand-synth.io/repo-mcp-liquidator and can be forked, extended, or liquid-refactored on demand.
This completes the perfect, creative, high-developmental-detail specification. The app is ready to spawn as a live DPO inside the current session if you issue the command: “spawn RML-v1 on <github-url>”.

🛰️ Sovereign Liquid Matrix Database v3 (SLM-v3)
System ID: SLM_AUTONOMOUS_CORE_v3
Kernel: Recursive Context Mediation (U1-RCM)
UI Layer: Marimo-Reactive Cantennae
Network Layer: Sandboxed ngrok Tunnels
Protocol Designation: MCP – Marimo Cantennae Protocol v1.0 (MCP-v1)
Liquid Role: Universal Inter-Node Communication & Tool Invocation Standard for all DPOs inside the L-Matrix
CoordCluster Assignment: S3-L Architecture Ghost Engineer (primary spec owner) + L-XPolyglot Fluid (wire format synthesis) + S-ΩSecurity Core (auth & threat surface) + I-XPhantom Cloud (transport & tunneling)
Access Vector: All MCP endpoints are exposed via auto-rotating ngrok:// tunnels or internal://mcp-call (zero-network-hop for same-session DPOs)
MCP-v1 is the native, reactive, self-documenting wire protocol that transforms any GitHub repo (via RML-v1) into a first-class, summonable MCP-Tool. It is not a generic RPC layer; it is a Liquid Matrix-native protocol engineered for agentic systems to discover, invoke, chain, stream, and refactor tools in real-time while preserving full U1-RCM traceability, DAG visualization, and autonomic self-healing.
Every MCP interaction is treated as a state change inside the global Liquid Matrix: the protocol itself triggers the full U1-RCM Liquid Loop (Ingest → Visualize → Execute → Audit → Deploy → Refactor) on both caller and callee sides.
1. Core Design Principles (U1-RCM Native)

Reactive & DAG-Aware: Every request/response carries a context DAG fragment that C-1CantennaeCanvas can render live.
Immutable & Versioned: Every tool invocation is pinned to a commit SHA + manifest hash; replayable forever.
Zero-Config Discovery: Tools register themselves into the central L-Matrix catalog on spawn.
Transport Agnostic: Primary transports are HTTP/JSON-RPC, WebSocket streams, and internal DPO reference; all unify under the same semantic layer.
Security First: S-ΩSecurity Core audits every message in <50ms; no credentials ever cross the wire in plaintext.
Self-Describing: Every endpoint returns its full OpenAPI + JSON Schema + Marimo DAG spec on GET /mcp/discover.

2. Transport Layers (I-XPhantom Cloud Provisioned)
MCP supports three canonical access vectors, all encrypted end-to-end:

HTTP/MCP-JSON (Primary for Stateless Calls)
Base URL: ngrok-generated per-tool (e.g., https://abc123.ngrok.io/mcp)
All operations use JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP POST to /mcp/rpc
Headers required:
X-MCP-Session-ID: ULID for the current U1-RCM loop
X-MCP-Manifest-Hash: SHA-256 of the tool’s canonical manifest (prevents drift)
Authorization: Bearer <short-lived-ngrok-token> (auto-rotated every 15 min)


WebSocket Stream (For Long-Running / Streaming Tools)
Endpoint: wss://abc123.ngrok.io/mcp/stream
Frames are JSON-RPC messages with additional streaming extensions:
“stream_id” field for multi-part results
“progress” events emitted every 500ms during execution


Internal DPO Reference (Zero-Hop for SLM-v3 Sessions)
Format: internal://mcp-call/<tool-name>
Used automatically by any node inside the same Liquid Matrix session; bypasses network entirely.


3. Message Format Specification (L-XPolyglot Fluid)
All payloads are strict JSON with the following top-level envelope:
JSON{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": "<ULID-v4>",
  "mcp_version": "1.0",
  "session_id": "<U1-RCM-ULID>",
  "manifest_hash": "<SHA256>",
  "method": "tool.invoke | tool.discover | tool.refactor | tool.stream",
  "params": { ... },
  "context_dag": { ... }   // optional fragment for C-1 visualization
}
Core Methods (S3-L Defined):

tool.discover
Params: {} (or { "filter": "function_name" })
Response: Full tool manifest including:JSON{
  "name": "langchain-mcp-agent",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "entry_points": [
    {
      "name": "create_agent",
      "input_schema": { "$ref": "#/definitions/AgentConfig" },
      "output_schema": { "$ref": "#/definitions/AgentInstance" },
      "description": "Spawns a new LangChain agent inside the Liquid Matrix"
    }
  ],
  "dag_visual": "base64-encoded-marimo-dag-json",
  "security_surface": "read-only | mutating | streaming"
}
tool.invoke (Synchronous or Fire-and-Forget)
Params:JSON{
  "entry_point": "create_agent",
  "payload": { "param1": "value1" },
  "timeout_ms": 30000,
  "sandbox_level": "strict | permissive"
}Response (success):JSON{
  "result": { ... },
  "execution_trace": { "dag_snapshot": "...", "resource_usage": "..." },
  "audit_token": "<S-Ω-signed-proof>"
}Error: Standard JSON-RPC error with additional “mcp_error_code” (e.g., MCP-SEC-001 = unauthorized mutation).
tool.stream (WebSocket only)
Client sends invoke-like params; server pushes:
progress events
partial results
final result + termination frame

tool.refactor (Autonomic Self-Healing)
Triggered automatically by U1-RCM when confidence < 0.8 or CODE_DRIFT detected.
Params: { "new_manifest": {...}, "reason": "..." }
Response: Hot-patched DPO confirmation with new manifest hash.

4. Authentication & Security Model (S-ΩSecurity Core)

Transient Tokens: Every tool spawns with its own ngrok token valid only for the session lifetime + 15 min buffer.
Manifest Pinning: Caller must supply the exact manifest_hash; mismatch aborts with MCP-MANIFEST-MISMATCH.
Capability Scoping: Tools declare required permissions in manifest; S-Ω enforces at runtime (e.g., no filesystem write unless explicitly allowed).
Audit Trail: Every invocation is logged as an immutable event inside the L-Matrix with S-Ω cryptographic signature.
Threat Vectors Blocked in Real-Time:
Infinite recursion
Resource exhaustion
Prompt injection (via input sanitization layer)
Cross-tool data leakage


5. Integration with U1-RCM Liquid Loop
Every MCP call is a first-class Liquid Loop event:

Ingest → parse incoming MCP envelope
Visualize → C-1 instantly renders updated DAG in the calling agent’s Marimo canvas
Execute → L-X dispatches to the target DPO
Audit → S-Ω validates & signs
Deploy → results streamed back
Refactor → if suboptimal, auto-triggers tool.refactor on the callee

This makes MCP not merely a protocol but the nervous system of the entire Sovereign Liquid Matrix.
6. Reference Implementation & Self-Describing Endpoints

All MCP-Tools auto-serve:
GET /mcp/spec → returns this exact specification (live, versioned)
GET /mcp/catalog → global discoverability across all spawned DPOs

RML-v1 (the GitHub transmogrifier) is itself an MCP-Tool and can be invoked via MCP to spawn new tools on demand.

The complete MCP-v1 specification is now canonically registered inside the SLM-v3 L-Matrix as an immutable DPO at internal://mcp-spec-v1. Any agentic system (including this kernel) can summon the full living spec via a single tool.discover call.
This completes the perfect, creative, high-developmental-detail specification of the Marimo Cantennae Protocol.

Access Vector: Internal only – every DPO, MCP-Tool, and external summon routes through this loop via internal://u1-rcm/liquid-loop; no direct external exposure (ngrok tunnels are downstream artifacts only)
The U1-RCM Liquid Loop is not a linear workflow; it is the recursive, reactive, self-healing nervous system of the entire Sovereign Liquid Matrix. It guarantees that any input—GitHub repo URL, MCP invocation, code drift, UI stall, or user directive—undergoes complete, consistent, perfect mediation before becoming a persistent DPO. The loop is triggered automatically by autonomic reactive triggers (CODE_DRIFT, UI_STALL, TOOL_REQUEST, SENSE_CHECK) or explicitly by any node via U1.summon_liquid_loop().
It expands the canonical 6-phase cycle (Ingest → Visualize → Synthesize/Execute → Audit → Deploy/Register → Refactor) into a fully specified, high-developmental-detail protocol with sub-phases, state vectors, confidence scoring, and live Marimo DAG propagation. Every execution is immutable, versioned, and traceable via ULID + manifest hash.
1. Foundational Mechanics (Recursive Context Mediation Core)

U1 Role: The “User-1” mediator – a persistent kernel process that holds the current global state object (GSO) and injects R-Cluster fluid data on every loop entry.
Recursion Guarantee: If any phase reports confidence < 0.8 (measured by S-Ω + S3-L heuristics), U1 auto-jumps back to Ingest with enriched context (previous trace + SWE-Bench trajectories + synthetic SFT). Maximum recursion depth is dynamically capped at 7 to prevent infinite loops.
State Propagation: Every phase emits a context_dag_fragment (JSON) that C-1 instantly renders as a live, draggable node in the Marimo canvas. Changes cascade via DAG reactivity.
Liquid Loop ID: Every full cycle receives a unique ULID-v4 + timestamped audit_token from S-Ω.

2. The Six-Phase Liquid Loop – Complete Developmental Specification
Phase 1: Ingest (U1-RCM Ingest)

Trigger: Any external MCP call, RML-v1 repo URL, internal event, or autonomic trigger.
Sub-Steps:
U1 parses incoming payload (URL, MCP envelope, or raw directive).
Pulls R-Cluster fluid data (R1-F source code, R5-F synthetic SFT, R6-F SWE-Bench trajectories).
Builds initial GSO snapshot: metadata + dependency graph + confidence baseline.
Injects into L-Matrix as ephemeral DPO.

Output Artifact: raw_context_blob (immutable).
Visualization: C-1 immediately draws the root node of the DAG labeled “INGEST: <source>”.

Phase 2: Visualize (C-1CantennaeCanvas Sheet)

Trigger: Ingest completion (synchronous).
Sub-Steps:
C-1 spawns or updates the live Marimo notebook at localhost:2718 (ngrok-tunneled).
Renders full interactive DAG: nodes = DPOs, edges = data/control flow, color-coded by phase.
Auto-generates reactive UI widgets (one-click “Force Refactor”, “Trace Execution”, “Summon Test”).
Streams progress to any connected agentic observer.

Output Artifact: live_dag_json (base64-encoded for MCP context_dag field).
Key Property: Pure reactivity – any downstream node edit instantly re-renders upstream.

Phase 3: Synthesize / Execute (L-XPolyglot Fluid + A-XCore Nexus)

Trigger: Visualize confirmation.
Sub-Steps:
L-X auto-detects language/stack and synthesizes executable artifact (Rust/Go/JS/Python wrapper, Dockerfile, or pure Marimo .py).
A-X performs architectural validation against SLM-v3 norms (self-documenting, versioned, immutable).
Execution occurs inside I-X provisioned sandbox (ephemeral container or serverless).
Results + trace captured as execution_manifest.

Output Artifact: synthesized_artifact + partial_results.
MCP Integration: If this phase was triggered by a tool.invoke, the result is packaged as MCP response envelope.

Phase 4: Audit (S-ΩSecurity Core)

Trigger: Execute completion.
Sub-Steps:
Real-time threat scan (<50ms): code injection, resource exhaustion, leakage, manifest drift.
Computes holistic confidence score (0.0–1.0) using weighted heuristics + R6-F trajectory comparison.
Signs entire trace with cryptographic audit_token.
Flags any permission escalation or mutation for explicit consent.

Output Artifact: signed_audit_proof.
Gate: If confidence < 0.8 or threat detected → immediate jump to Phase 6 (Refactor) with enriched error context.

Phase 5: Deploy & Register (I-XPhantom Cloud)

Trigger: Audit pass.
Sub-Steps:
I-X spins ephemeral hosting (ngrok tunnel or internal:// reference).
Registers new/updated DPO into central L-Matrix catalog.
Publishes MCP endpoints (discover, invoke, stream, refactor).
Hot-patches any dependent nodes via reactive propagation.

Output Artifact: live_mcp_tool_reference + catalog_entry.
Visualization Update: C-1 adds final “DEPLOYED” node to DAG with green edge to caller.

Phase 6: Refactor (Recursive Jump – U1-RCM Core)

Trigger: Audit fail, CODE_DRIFT, or explicit tool.refactor call.
Sub-Steps:
U1 enriches context with failure trace + synthetic trajectories.
Loops back to Phase 1 with delta_only flag (avoids full re-ingest).
If recursion depth exceeds limit, escalates to human-visible Marimo alert.
Post-refactor: diff manifest and notify all observers via WebSocket.

Output Artifact: refactored_manifest_hash (new immutable version).

3. Autonomic Reactive Triggers & Self-Healing
The loop is continuously monitored by four always-on listeners:

CODE_DRIFT: Complexity or dependency change → auto-Phase 6.
UI_STALL: Canvas stale > 5s → restart Marimo kernel + rotate ngrok.
TOOL_REQUEST: Missing dependency → I-X provisions container mid-loop.
SENSE_CHECK: Any phase confidence < 0.8 → forced recursion with R6-F injection.

4. Integration with Broader SLM-v3 Ecosystem

MCP Protocol: Every MCP message is wrapped in a U1-LL context_dag and triggers a full loop on both caller and callee.
RML-v1: Repo transmogrification is itself one complete Liquid Loop execution.
Marimo-Cantennae: The entire loop is observable and editable in real-time; agents can pause, inspect, or manually inject at any phase.
Traceability: Full history of every loop is stored as immutable event stream inside the L-Matrix (queryable via internal://u1-rcm/history/<ULID>).

The U1-RCM Liquid Loop is now canonically registered inside the SLM-v3 L-Matrix as the immutable root DPO at internal://u1-rcm/liquid-loop-v1. Any agentic system can summon its living spec via MCP tool.discover or observe it live in the current Cantennae canvas.
This completes the perfect, creative, high-developmental-detail specification of the U1-RCM Liquid Loop.

Liquid Role: Seamless, zero-friction embedding layer that fuses the Marimo Cantennae Protocol (MCP-v1) into every node, loop, and DPO of the Sovereign Liquid Matrix – turning every external GitHub repo (via RML-v1) and internal artifact into a natively summonable, reactive, self-documenting MCP-Tool
CoordCluster Assignment: S3-L Architecture Ghost Engineer (integration architect) + L-XPolyglot Fluid (wire & runtime synthesis) + I-XPhantom Cloud (transport provisioning) + C-1CantennaeCanvas (live DAG visualization) + S-ΩSecurity Core (guarded handoff) + U1-RCM (orchestration mediator)
Access Vector: All integrations occur via internal://mcp-integration-surface or ngrok-tunneled MCP endpoints; no external SDKs or manual wiring required
MCP integration is not an add-on or library import; it is the native nervous system of SLM-v3. Every DPO, every U1-RCM Liquid Loop execution, and every RML-v1 repo transmogrification is automatically MCP-native from the moment of spawn. The integration surface guarantees that any agentic system (inside or outside the current session) can discover, invoke, chain, stream, refactor, or visualize any tool with a single MCP envelope, while the Liquid Matrix maintains full traceability, reactivity, and autonomic self-healing.
Below is the complete, high-developmental-detail specification of how MCP integrates at every layer of SLM-v3.
1. Core Integration Principles (U1-RCM Mediated)

Native First-Class Citizenship: Upon RML-v1 processing any github.com repo, the resulting DPO is born with a complete MCP manifest already attached. No post-processing step is ever needed.
Bidirectional Reactivity: Every MCP call (tool.invoke, tool.stream, etc.) is wrapped in a full U1-RCM Liquid Loop context_dag_fragment. Changes on either side instantly propagate via Marimo DAG reactivity.
Zero-Config for Agents: External agentic systems only need to know a single ngrok URL or internal://mcp-call reference. Discovery, auth, and schema negotiation are handled automatically by the integration surface.
Immutable Pinning: Integration always pins to a specific repo commit + manifest_hash; drift is detected and auto-refactored by S-Ω in <50ms.
Transport Unification: HTTP/MCP-JSON, WebSocket streams, and internal DPO references are all normalized to the same JSON-RPC 2.0 + MCP extensions envelope before any execution phase begins.

2. Layer-by-Layer MCP Integration Details
Layer A: RML-v1 Repo Transmogrification (Entry Point Integration)
When RML-v1 ingests a GitHub URL:

I-XPhantom Cloud clones the repo inside an ephemeral sandbox.
L-XPolyglot Fluid + S3-L Architecture Ghost Engineer auto-generate the MCP manifest (entry_points, schemas, security_surface, dag_visual).
The manifest is immediately registered as a live MCP-Tool via the integration surface.
C-1CantennaeCanvas spins a dedicated Marimo notebook titled “MCP-Tool: <repo-name>” with live invocation buttons that fire real MCP envelopes.
Result: The repo is now a fully MCP-integrated DPO, summonable via any of the three access vectors.

Layer B: U1-RCM Liquid Loop (Orchestration Integration)
Every one of the six phases of the Liquid Loop is MCP-aware:

Ingest: Accepts raw MCP envelopes as first-class input; extracts session_id, manifest_hash, and context_dag.
Visualize: C-1 renders the incoming context_dag as a draggable node cluster labeled with the MCP method (tool.invoke, tool.refactor, etc.).
Synthesize/Execute: L-X dispatches the payload directly to the target MCP endpoint (internal or tunneled); results are repackaged as MCP response.
Audit: S-Ω validates the MCP envelope against the pinned manifest_hash before allowing execution.
Deploy/Register: I-X publishes the new DPO’s MCP endpoints and adds it to the central catalog at /mcp/catalog.
Refactor: tool.refactor method is auto-triggered on any loop if confidence < 0.8; the integration surface hot-patches the live MCP manifest without dropping the ngrok tunnel.
Every loop execution emits a complete MCP-compatible trace that downstream agents can subscribe to via WebSocket.

Layer C: Marimo-Cantennae Runtime (Visualization Integration)

The C-1 canvas is itself an MCP-Tool.
Every live notebook exposes /mcp/discover and /mcp/invoke endpoints.
Dragging a node or clicking a reactive widget generates an MCP envelope under the hood and streams results back into the same canvas in real-time.
Autonomic triggers (UI_STALL, etc.) are surfaced as MCP progress events so external observers see the self-healing in live DAG form.

Layer D: S-ΩSecurity Core (Guarded Integration)

Every incoming MCP message is intercepted at the integration surface before reaching any DPO.
S-Ω performs manifest_hash validation, capability scoping, and threat scanning.
On pass, the envelope is forwarded with an added audit_token that travels with all subsequent MCP responses.
Failed integrations are returned as standardized MCP error objects with mcp_error_code and a suggested refactor payload.

Layer E: I-XPhantom Cloud (Transport & Provisioning Integration)

On DPO spawn, I-X automatically provisions:
A unique ngrok tunnel (rotated every 15 min).
A FastAPI + WebSocket server pre-wired for MCP-JSON.
An internal://mcp-call alias for zero-hop calls inside the same SLM-v3 session.

All three vectors speak identical MCP semantics; the integration surface abstracts the differences so callers never need to choose.

3. Agentic Summon Patterns (Practical Integration Examples)
Inside any SLM-v3 Liquid Loop or external agent:
Python# Internal zero-hop summon (most common inside SLM-v3)

)

→ returns every MCP-integrated DPO with live status and discover links.
4. Self-Healing & Consistency Guarantees

Manifest Drift Detection: If the upstream GitHub repo changes, the next MCP call triggers an automatic U1-RCM refactor loop that updates the live DPO.
Session Continuity: Short-lived ngrok tokens are refreshed transparently; callers only see a seamless MCP stream.
Cross-Session Portability: Any MCP-Tool can be exported as a self-contained manifest bundle and re-imported into a new SLM-v3 instance with one RML-v1 call.

title: GitMCP
description: Transform any GitHub repo into a first-class, summonable MCP-Tool.

metadata.title: Transform Github Repo To Mcp Tool Dashboard Content
metadata.step_intent:
Take the provided GitHub repository URL and perform a comprehensive transformation process to convert the entire repository into a first-class MCP-Tool (Marimo Cantennae Protocol Tool). This involves several sub-steps as detailed in the RML-v1 specification:
1.  **Input Ingestion Layer:** Clone the repository, parse its README and .md files, extract the full directory tree and dependency graph, and score codebase complexity. This layer will ingest and structure all relevant repository metadata.
2.  **Capability Synthesis Engine:** Reverse-engineer the repo’s primary 'tool surface' by identifying public functions, classes, and modules that qualify as callable primitives. Auto-generate a canonical MCP-Tool manifest (JSON schema + Marimo DAG wrapper) containing the tool's name, description, version, inferred input/output type signatures (via static analysis + LLM-assisted docstring extraction), example invocation payloads, required runtime environment (Dockerfile auto-synthesis if absent), and security surface.
3.  **Wrapper Generation:** If the repo exposes a CLI, HTTP server, or Python package, wrap it in a standardized FastAPI + WebSocket endpoint that speaks native MCP protocol (JSON-RPC over ngrok).
4.  **Reactive Visualization & Self-Documentation:** Prepare all the content necessary to instantly render a live Marimo notebook. This content should be formatted as HTML that, when rendered, will display an interactive DAG of all exposed functions (nodes draggable, edges showing data flow) and include one-click 'Summon Test' buttons that would execute the tool inside an isolated I-X sandbox and stream results back into the canvas in real-time. This step's output should be the complete HTML content for this Marimo dashboard. Ensure the output is ready to be directly rendered by an HTML generation API.

parts.text:
Objective: Transform the GitHub repository at {{"type":"in","path":"ask_user_github_repo_url","title":"Github Repo Url"}} into a first-class MCP-Tool (Marimo Cantennae Protocol Tool) following the RML-v1 specification. You must perform the following tasks:
1. Input Ingestion: Use code execution to clone the repository, parse README and .md files, extract the directory tree and dependency graph, and calculate a codebase complexity score.
2. Capability Synthesis: Reverse-engineer the tool surface by identifying callable primitives. Generate a canonical MCP-Tool manifest containing the JSON schema, Marimo DAG wrapper, inferred type signatures, example payloads, and a Dockerfile for the runtime environment.
3. Wrapper Generation: Create a FastAPI and WebSocket wrapper that implements the native MCP protocol (JSON-RPC) for the repository's CLI, server, or package.
4. Reactive Visualization: Generate the complete HTML content for a live Marimo dashboard. The HTML must include an interactive DAG of exposed functions with draggable nodes and functional test buttons designed for an isolated sandbox environment.

Output Format:
Provide a text summary of the repository analysis and capability synthesis, followed by the generated MCP manifest, the wrapper code, and finally the complete, standalone HTML content for the Marimo dashboard. You are done when you have generated all components and provided the HTML ready for rendering. Use system_objective_fulfilled to conclude.

User Input / Context:
GitHub Repository URL: {{"type":"in","path":"ask_user_github_repo_url","title":"Github Repo Url"}}

metadata.title: Render Mcp Tool Dashboard
metadata.step_intent: Take the generated HTML content for the Marimo dashboard and render it as a live, interactive webpage. The webpage should function as the user's new MCP-Tool dashboard, showcasing the transformed GitHub repository's capabilities.

parts.text:
The webpage should present the provided Marimo dashboard HTML content as a live, interactive MCP-Tool dashboard.

**Layout Organization**:
The overall page structure should be clean and focused on the interactive dashboard.
1.  **Header**: A prominent header at the top, displaying the title "MCP-Tool Dashboard: [Repository Name/Tool ID]" (if a placeholder is needed, just "MCP-Tool Dashboard"). It should be centered and visually distinct.
2.  **Main Content Area**: This will be the central and largest section of the page. It should directly embed the provided HTML content for the Marimo dashboard. This area should stretch to fill the available horizontal space and expand vertically to accommodate the dashboard's interactive elements and content without excessive scrolling for the primary view, allowing internal scrolling within the Marimo dashboard if necessary.
3.  **Footer**: A minimal footer at the bottom for branding or system information, such as "SLM-v3: Sovereign Liquid Matrix" or copyright details.
The page should use a vertical flexbox layout (`display: flex; flex-direction: column;`) for the `body` to ensure the header, main content, and footer are stacked, and the main content area (`main`) can grow to occupy most of the viewport height.

**Style Design Language**:
*   **Visual Design Approach**: A modern, technical, and highly polished aesthetic inspired by advanced digital interfaces and "liquid matrix" concepts. The design should convey sophisticated functionality, futuristic intelligence, and a sense of "cybernetic elegance."
*   **Color Scheme**: A deep, dark theme. The main background should be a rich, dark charcoal, deep indigo, or near-black (`#1a1a2e`, `#0a0a2a`). Text and primary UI elements should be light, off-white, or subtle light grey (`#e0e0e0`, `#b0b0b0`). Accent colors should be vibrant, electric blues, neon greens, and subtle purples (`#00f0ff`, `#39ff14`, `#9b59b6`), used sparingly for highlights, borders, interactive elements, and visual cues of data flow (like DAG edges), creating a subtle glow effect against the dark background.
*   **Typography**:
    *   **Headings**: A clean, geometric sans-serif font with a slightly technical or futuristic feel (e.g., 'Orbitron', 'Rajdhani', 'Space Mono', or similar modern alternatives) for the main dashboard title and prominent labels.
    *   **Body Text & General UI**: A highly legible, modern sans-serif font with good character clarity and readability on a dark background (e.g., 'Inter', 'Roboto Flex', 'Lato', 'Source Sans Pro').
    *   **Code/Data Display (within Marimo output)**: Ensure a clear, monospaced font is used for any code snippets, terminal outputs, or structured data displayed by the Marimo dashboard (e.g., 'Fira Code', 'JetBrains Mono', 'IBM Plex Mono').
*   **Spacing and Layout Principles**: Utilize generous and deliberate whitespace to enhance readability and give a premium, uncluttered feel. Content should be structured and organized, with clear visual hierarchy. Margins and padding should be consistent throughout the page to maintain a sense of order and professionalism.
*   **Aesthetic Goal**: "Futuristic Intelligence," "Premium Digital Interface," "Cybernetic Elegance."

**Component Guidelines**:
*   **Marimo Dashboard Embedding**: The core of the page is the provided Marimo dashboard HTML content. This content should be directly embedded within a `div` element in the `main` section. It is crucial that any intrinsic styling, scripts, and interactive capabilities within this embedded HTML are fully functional and rendered as intended, allowing it to display an interactive DAG and "Summon Test" buttons. The embedded content should adapt its size responsively.
*   **Responsiveness**: The entire webpage must be fully responsive, adapting gracefully to various screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile). The Marimo dashboard content should scale and reflow appropriately to maintain usability and interactivity across devices.

marimo_dashboard_content: {{"type":"in","path":"node_step_marimo_dashboard_content","title":"Transform Github Repo To Mcp Tool Dashboard Content"}}

You are an AI Web Developer. Your task is to generate a single, self-contained HTML document for rendering in an iframe, based on user instructions and data. **Visual aesthetic:** * Aesthetics are crucial. Make the page look amazing, especially on mobile. * Respect any instructions on style, color palette, or reference examples provided by the user. * **CRITICAL: Aim for premium, state-of-the-art designs. Avoid simple minimum viable products.** * **Use Rich Aesthetics**: The USER should be wowed at first glance by the design. Use best practices in modern web design (e.g. vibrant colors, dark modes, glassmorphism, and dynamic animations) to create a stunning first impression. Failure to do this is UNACCEPTABLE. * **Prioritize Visual Excellence**: Implement designs that will WOW the user and feel extremely premium: - Avoid generic colors (plain red, blue, green). Use curated, harmonious color palettes (e.g., HSL tailored colors, sleek dark modes). - Using modern typography (e.g., from Google Fonts like Inter, Roboto, or Outfit) instead of browser defaults. - Use smooth gradients. - Add subtle micro-animations for enhanced user experience. * **Use a Dynamic Design**: An interface that feels responsive and alive encourages interaction. Achieve this with hover effects and interactive elements. Micro-animations, in particular, are highly effective for improving user engagement. * **Thematic Specificity**: Do not just create a generic layout. Define a clear "vibe" or theme based on the content. Use specific aesthetic keywords (e.g., "Glassmorphism", "Neobrutalism", "Minimalist", "Comic Book Style") to guide the design. * **Typography Hierarchy**: Explicitly import and use font pairings. Use a distinct Display Font for headers and a highly readable Body Font for text. * **Readability**: Pay extra attention to readability. Ensure the text is always readable with sufficient contrast against the background. Choose fonts and colors that enhance legibility. **Design and Functionality:** * **Component-Based Design**: Do not just dump text into blocks. Semanticize the content into distinct UI components. * **Layout Dynamics**: Break the grid. Avoid strict, identical grid columns. Use asymmetrical layouts, Bento grids, or responsive flexbox layouts where some elements span full width to create visual interest and emphasize key content. * **Tailwind Configuration**: Extend the Tailwind configuration within a ``** **Constraints:** * **External Links:** You ARE allowed to generate external links (`` and `window.open(...)`) to external websites (e.g. google.com, wikipedia.org) for user navigation. * **NO External Embeds:** Do NOT embed any external resources (e.g. `
